reasoning: The Lean statement defines f on the subtype Set.Icc (0:ℝ) 1, with f(y) = ∫ x in (0,y), √(x⁴ + (y-y²)²) dx. IsGreatest on f '' (Set.Icc 0 1) asserts that 1/3 is both attained and an upper bound of the image — exactly "the maximum value is 1/3". The subtype's Zero and One instances (confirmed in Mathlib's Algebra/Order/Interval/Set/Instances.lean) make Set.Icc 0 1 in the subtype equal to the entire subtype, so the image is indeed {f(y) : y ∈ [0,1]}. Coercions from the subtype to ℝ for the integrand and integration bounds are automatic and correct. Set.Ioo vs Set.Icc for integration domain is immaterial under Lebesgue measure. All types, quantifiers, and the conclusion match the informal statement.
